what_it_does: This file is a JSON change ledger that records, per project, the counts and relative paths of files that were added, changed, deleted, or left unchanged in a single snapshot.
observations: Three projects are listed: company, counterpart-design, and claude-ds.
The company project has 259 added files, 62 changed files, 1 deleted file, and 9294 unchanged files.
company ledger_total is 9421 while tree_total is 15821, so many tree files are not represented in the ledger counts.
counterpart-design has no changes at all; all 1576 files are unchanged.
claude-ds has 5 added files (mostly under _demo) and 2 changed files.
Many new files are in build-prep/the-one-system, build-prep/openwebui-company-fusion, build-prep/operator-surface, channel-memory/noticeboard, runtime/capabilities, ops/ledger_interpret*, and voice/.
A large number of added files are channel-memory/noticeboard/item-*.md entries with hexadecimal IDs.
Several AGENTS.md files appear as added or changed across guides, introspection, modes, platforms, roles, runtime, and voice/ears.
The single deleted file is guides/using-skills.py while guides/using_skills.py was added, suggesting a hyphen-to-underscore rename.
New binary voice model files (decoder.int8.onnx, encoder.int8.onnx, joiner.int8.onnx) are included in the added list.
standouts: The ledger_total vs tree_total gap in the company project.
The large batch of channel-memory/noticeboard item files.
The addition of a full ledger interpretation pipeline (ops/ledger_interpret*.py and .sh).
The apparent rename of guides/using-skills.py to guides/using_skills.py.
The claude-ds project adding a _demo subdirectory and face-index.js.
concerns: tree_total exceeds ledger_total by a large margin with no explanation of why those files are excluded.
No timestamp, commit hash, or snapshot identifier is provided.
Binary model files are listed without size or checksum metadata.
A deleted/added path pair looks like a rename but is not explicitly linked.
notes: A project-scoped diff summary used to feed an interpretive layer, dominated by new substrates in the company project.
questions: What is the exact difference between ledger_total and tree_total?
What commit, date, or snapshot does this change set represent?
Why is counterpart-design included if it has zero changes?
Is guides/using-skills.py a rename to guides/using_skills.py?
Are the channel-memory/noticeboard items auto-generated, and what do they represent?
purpose_vs_actual: Partial match: it catalogs changes with counts and path lists, but it does not yet contain prose interpretations or annotations beyond raw categorization.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to give downstream tooling a concise, machine-readable summary of what is new, modified, or removed so an interpretive layer can reason about project evolution and fusion scope.
novelty: The combination of code, design docs, HTML mockups, systemd units, ONNX binaries, and channel-memory items in a single per-project change ledger, plus the ledger_total/tree_total distinction.
contribution: A normalized, project-scoped inventory of added, changed, deleted, and unchanged substrates that a fused system can query to discover recent work without scanning the entire tree.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON change ledger listing per-project counts and relative paths of added, changed, deleted, and unchanged files across the company, counterpart-design, and claude-ds projects.
intention_for_embedding: To capture a snapshot of recent modifications and new substrates so an interpretive layer can summarize project evolution and scope.
